concept: Strong / Having Power / Able to Withstand
description: Having the power to move, lift, or withstand great force; powerful in effect or intensity. 'Kema' names the quality of capable force—muscles that can lift, materials that hold, presence that cannot be ignored.
sound_symbolism: The word holds firm. 'Ke' begins with solid contact, force established. Then 'ma' embodies the power, strength made flesh.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'kema shiro' (strong tree). Neutral term for power—can describe physical strength, emotional fortitude, or material durability.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: True strength includes gentleness—the strong can afford to be soft, while the weak must be rigid.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Strength came from work and necessity—bodies strengthened by labor, communities strengthened by cooperation.
